name: Ascent to Los Rehoyos (Sierra de la Solana) — circular from Solana del Pino
name_original: Subida a Los Rehoyos (Sierra de la Solana) — circular desde Solana del Pino
region_intro: Jogging routes around Pastos are set against a dramatic backdrop of Andean landscapes, featuring prominent volcanoes and serene lagoons. The region's high elevation, around 2,500 meters above sea level, provides a challenging yet rewarding environment for runners. Terrain varies from Andean forests to páramos, with routes often utilizing roads and paths through scenic rural areas. Best jogging routes around Pastos The most popular jogging route is Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain suitable for a steady run. Another top favourite among local runners is Cerro de San Agustín by the Laguna de Almodóvar — circular from Puertollano, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This route offers a challenging workout with significant elevation gain and scenic views of the Laguna de Almodóvar. Local runners also love the Ascent to Los Rehoyos (Sierra de la Solana) — circular from Solana del Pino, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Pastos is defined by volcanic landscapes, high-altitude lagoons, and Andean forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate trails to more difficult ascents. The routes in Pastos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Pastos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Pastos?
A: There are over 60 dedicated running routes around Pastos, offering a wide variety of terrain and difficulty levels for all types of runners. The komoot community has explored 65 routes in the region.

Q: What makes running in Pastos unique?
A: Running in Pastos offers a truly unique experience due to its dramatic Andean landscapes, high-altitude environment (around 2,500 meters above sea level), and the presence of prominent volcanoes like Galeras and serene lagoons such as Laguna de la Cocha. The diverse terrain ranges from Andean forests to páramos, providing both challenging and rewarding runs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Pastos?
A: Yes, Pastos offers some easier options for those new to running or looking for a more relaxed pace. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least 1 easy route available. For a moderate yet popular option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 miles long and takes about 55 minutes.

Q: Where can I find challenging long-distance running trails near Pastos?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Pastos has 20 difficult routes. The region's volcanic terrain, including areas around Galeras and Azufral volcanoes, offers strenuous ascents and significant elevation gains. A notable difficult route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210094153/cerro-de-san-agustin-by-the-laguna-de-almodovar-circular-from-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Agustín by the Laguna de Almodóvar — circular from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.7-mile path with scenic views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304437503/ascent-to-los-rehoyos-sierra-de-la-solana-circular-from-solana-del-pino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Los Rehoyos (Sierra de la Solana) — circular from Solana del Pino&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.2 miles long and features mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Pastos?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Pastos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210094153/cerro-de-san-agustin-by-the-laguna-de-almodovar-circular-from-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Agustín by the Laguna de Almodóvar — circular from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints or natural attractions?
A: Absolutely! Pastos is rich in natural beauty. Many routes offer stunning views of the Andean mountains and volcanoes. You can find trails that lead to or pass by attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, or offer glimpses of the dramatic Galeras Volcano.

Q: Are there running trails that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, if you enjoy running near waterfalls, you're in luck. The region features the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lastras&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that includes a waterfall.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Pastos?
A: The running routes in Pastos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 400 visitors. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the breathtaking volcanic and lagoon landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the running routes?
A: Yes, some running routes in Pastos offer a chance to explore historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt;, offering a cultural element to your run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on a run around Pastos?
A: The terrain around Pastos is incredibly diverse, reflecting its Andean and volcanic nature. You can expect to run through Andean forests, open páramos, and along paths that offer views of volcanic landscapes. Many routes utilize existing roads and trails through scenic rural areas, providing a mix of surfaces and gradients.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Laguna de Almodóvar?
A: Yes, for stunning views of the Laguna de Almodóvar, consider the challenging route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210094153/cerro-de-san-agustin-by-the-laguna-de-almodovar-circular-from-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Agustín by the Laguna de Almodóvar — circular from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;. This 9.7-mile trail provides a rewarding workout with picturesque lake scenery.
tips: On the southern side of the Sierra de la Solana is this small town surrounded by immense forests and with beautiful views of the northern Sierra Madrona.
Los Rehoyos is a wooded mountain located on the north side of Solana del Pino and from this point there are beautiful views of the town.
